name: Example app OIDC auth-code persistence update
outcome: The example app persists nonce and auth_time on authorization codes so it remains a safe template for opaque-code OIDC consumers.
scope: Update the example app's auth-code entity, persistence layer, and schema/migration artifacts to include the nonce and auth_time fields introduced by issue 3. No core library behavior changes.
acceptance_criteria: The example app OAuthAuthCode model/schema includes nullable nonce and auth_time fields matching the library entity shape
The example app repository persists and reloads nonce/auth_time for opaque authorization codes
An example-app test or smoke fixture proves nonce survives an authorize-to-token round trip when opaque codes are enabled
Docs or inline example comments call out that opaque-code repositories must persist nonce/auth_time for OIDC
steps: example app schema/model — add nullable nonce and auth_time fields to the auth-code persistence shape introduced by issue 3
example app auth-code repository — persist and hydrate nonce/auth_time when issuing and resolving opaque authorization codes
example app tests/fixtures — add an opaque-code OIDC nonce round-trip assertion so the example cannot regress into the fail-loud path
example app docs/comments — note that JWT auth codes avoid this persistence obligation while opaque-code repositories must store nonce/auth_time
notes: Split out of issue 9 during PRD review because example persistence is concrete data work and should not be buried in the final docs/API integration slice. Depends on issue 3 for the final field names and guard behavior.
